fr:title.text: From initial weak algebras to initial successor algebras ›

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: From initial weak algebras to initial successor algebras ›

fr:mainmatter.html:p: Let X be a successor algebra; we have shown that weak algebra homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{\iota }}{s\cdot X} from I to the successor restriction of X also tracks a successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{\iota }}{X}. To see that this homomorphism is unique, fix any successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}; we have also shown that such a homomorphism also tracks a (weak algebra) homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X}. But {I}\xrightarrow {{\iota }}{s\cdot X} is the such weak algebra homomorphism, so we have \iota = j.

fr:title.text: From initial weak algebras to initial successor algebras

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: From initial weak algebras to initial successor algebras

fr:mainmatter.html:p: Let I be the initial weak algebra for a monad \mathbb {T} on a category \mathcal {C}. Then the induced successor algebra \bar {I} is the initial successor algebra for \mathbb {T}.

Let X be a successor algebra; we have shown that weak algebra homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{\iota }}{s\cdot X} from I to the successor restriction of X also tracks a successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{\iota }}{X}. To see that this homomorphism is unique, fix any successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}; we have also shown that such a homomorphism also tracks a (weak algebra) homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X}. But {I}\xrightarrow {{\iota }}{s\cdot X} is the such weak algebra homomorphism, so we have \iota = j.

fr:title.text: From successor algebra homomorphisms to weak algebra homomorphisms

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: From successor algebra homomorphisms to weak algebra homomorphisms

fr:mainmatter.html:p: Let \mathbb {T} \equiv {\mathopen {}\left (T,\eta ,\mu \right )\mathclose {}} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C} and let I be an invertible weak algebra for \mathbb {T}; let X be an arbitrary successor algebra. Then a successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X} also tracks a weak algebra homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X}, where \bar {I} is the induced successor algebra of I and s\cdot X is the successor restriction of X.

fr:title.text: Lifting homomorphisms to the induced successor algebra

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Lifting homomorphisms to the induced successor algebra

fr:mainmatter.html:p: Let \mathbb {T} \equiv {\mathopen {}\left (T,\eta ,\mu \right )\mathclose {}} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C} and let I be an invertible weak algebra for \mathbb {T}; let X be an arbitrary successor algebra. Then a homomorphism {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X} also tracks a successor algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}, where \bar {I} is the induced successor algebra of I and s\cdot X is the successor restriction of X.
We have already seen that {I}\xrightarrow {{j}}{s\cdot X} tracks an algebra homomorphism {\bar {I}}\xrightarrow {{j}}{X}. It remains to show that this homomorphism preserves the successor operation.
A homomorphism of successor algebras is a monad algebra homomorphism {X}\xrightarrow {{h}}{Y} that preserves the successor operation:

fr:title.text: The successor restriction of a successor algebra

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The successor restriction of a successor algebra

fr:mainmatter.html:p: Let \mathbb {T} be a monad on a category \mathcal {C}, and let X be an successor algebra for \mathbb {T}. We define the successor restriction of X to be the weak algebra s\cdot X :\equiv s_{X}\cdot X defined by restricting X along its successor map.
We shall refer to an algebra for the underlying endofunctor of a monad as a weak algebra, following Joyal and Moerdijk.
